assumptions: The developer has a supported browser and an accessible email inbox.
No Salesforce org, Developer Edition credentials, Connected App, or CLI auth session exist yet.
The developer has not preselected Apex, Lightning, GraphQL, Bulk, Marketing, Commerce, Data Cloud, or another non-REST surface.

developer_goal: Create a Salesforce developer account and reach the earliest meaningful first-success outcome for a chosen Salesforce development surface.

first_success.outcome: For the REST API Quick Start candidate: authenticate a free Developer Edition org and complete the documented Account BillingCity update verified in Salesforce.
first_success.observable_signal: The PATCH request returns HTTP 204 No Content and the Account BillingCity shows as Fremont after refresh, when the REST Quick Start is the selected route.

route_selection.surface: Salesforce Developers self-service web and CLI surfaces
route_selection.rule: Select the shortest complete route only after the Salesforce product surface and first-success terminal are fixed.

candidates.name: REST API Quick Start with free Developer Edition and Salesforce CLI auth
candidates.reason: Official and complete within REST API, but Salesforce does not claim it as the universal first-mile across Apex, Lightning, GraphQL, Bulk, and other surfaces.

candidates.name: Apex, Lightning, GraphQL, Bulk, Marketing, Commerce, or Data Cloud first mile
candidates.reason: Each surface has different tools, entitlements, and terminal outcomes that cannot be compared as one Salesforce path.

candidates.name: Existing production org or sandbox instead of Developer Edition
candidates.reason: Uses a different account and entitlement boundary than the free Developer Edition signup route.

required_path.action: Open the Salesforce Developer Edition signup form and complete the visible identity and company prompts.

required_fields.label: Main Services Agreement, Salesforce Program Agreement, and API Terms of Service
Developer Edition AI features and inactivity acknowledgement
Remaining anti-abuse or username controls
required_fields.notes: Cold fetch exposed identity, email, and agreement controls; CAPTCHA, username uniqueness, or post-submit challenges still need a complete walkthrough.

required_path.observable_result: Salesforce accepts the signup submission and begins Developer Edition provisioning.

required_path.action: Open the Developer Edition activation or welcome email and follow the verification link.
required_path.observable_result: The developer reaches password and security-question setup for the new org.

required_path.action: Set the Developer Edition password, complete the security challenge, and sign in.
required_path.observable_result: The developer is signed in to a Developer Edition org with API Enabled permission available on the profile.

required_path.action: Install Salesforce CLI if needed, run sf org login web, and allow the CLI connected app for the Developer Edition org.
required_path.observable_result: Salesforce CLI holds an access token for the Developer Edition org.

required_path.action: Run the REST Quick Start discovery, Account query, and BillingCity PATCH sequence with cURL or an equivalent REST client.
required_path.observable_result: The PATCH returns HTTP 204 and the Account BillingCity update is visible in Salesforce.

prerequisites.description: An accessible email inbox is required to activate the Developer Edition org.
The user profile must have the API Enabled permission before REST calls succeed.

external_gates.description: Developer Edition signup requires accepting Salesforce developer and API program terms on the live form.
CLI authentication requires authorizing Salesforce CLI as a connected app in the browser.

unavoidable_waits.description: Salesforce must deliver the activation or welcome email and provision the Developer Edition org before first login.

platform_outcomes.description: Salesforce provisions a free Developer Edition org after signup and verification.
Salesforce CLI authentication yields an access token usable for REST requests.
A successful Account PATCH returns HTTP 204 and persists the BillingCity change.

excluded.item: Reading REST API conceptual documentation beyond the Quick Start steps
excluded.reason: Documentation reading is evidence, not a measured product action.

excluded.item: Creating a custom Connected App instead of using Salesforce CLI
excluded.reason: The Quick Start documents Salesforce CLI as the convenience auth path that requires no Connected App configuration.

excluded.item: Apex, Lightning, GraphQL, Bulk, Marketing, Commerce, or Data Cloud onboarding
excluded.reason: Those are alternate product surfaces, not part of the REST Quick Start candidate.

sources.title: REST API Quick Start
evidence_supported: Developer Edition signup as step one

sources.title: Sign up for your Developer Edition
evidence_supported: First name, last name, job title, company, country/region, and work email
MSA / Program Agreement / API Terms checkbox

sources.title: Step One: Sign up for Salesforce Developer Edition
Step Three: Walk Through the Sample Code

uncertainties.question: Which Salesforce product surface should define the organization-wide first mile: REST API, Apex, Lightning, GraphQL, Bulk, Marketing, Commerce, Data Cloud, or another surface?
uncertainties.impact: The choice changes required tools, fields, permissions, and the observable first-success terminal.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A bounded developer goal that names one surface, then a cold walkthrough of that surface from account creation to its official first success.

uncertainties.question: Do CAPTCHA, username uniqueness, or other post-submit challenges appear after the observed Developer Edition signup fields for a fresh participant?
uncertainties.impact: Additional anti-abuse or username controls would change the verified required-field count even for the REST candidate.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A dated full-page screenshot or read-only walkthrough of developer.salesforce.com/signup through successful submission for a new identity.
